title: Time and Daily Life Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The first month of the year.
choices: January
February
April
rounds.answer: January
rounds.explanation: January: The first month of the year.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The seventh month of the year, often very warm.
choices: January
February
July
rounds.answer: July
rounds.explanation: July: The seventh month of the year, often very warm.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A paper or digital tool that shows the days, weeks and months of a year.
choices: February
calendar
January
rounds.answer: calendar
rounds.explanation: calendar: A paper or digital tool that shows the days, weeks and months of a year.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The second month of the year, and the shortest with twenty-eight days.
rounds.answer: february

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The eighth month of the year, and a common time for family travel.
rounds.answer: august

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A period of one hundred years.
rounds.answer: century

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The school year begins in January.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: We plant our garden in April.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: We go to visit family in July.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: How many minutes make one hour?
choices: Twenty-four.
One hundred.
Sixty.
Three hundred and sixty-five.
rounds.answer: Sixty.
rounds.explanation: An hour is sixty minutes. Twenty-four is the hours in one day and three hundred and sixty-five is the days in one year.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses the Past Simple correctly?
choices: Last year we goed to the beach.
Last year we go to the beach.
Last year we are going to the beach very early in the morning.
Last year we went to the beach.
rounds.answer: Last year we went to the beach.
rounds.explanation: Go is irregular, so its past form is went. Goed does not exist, and the other two are present forms.

rounds.prompt: Which one of these sentences is an imperative?
choices: I prepare my calendar every Sunday.
Prepare your calendar.
She prepared her calendar last night.
Are you preparing your calendar this evening?
rounds.answer: Prepare your calendar.
rounds.explanation: An imperative starts with the base verb and has no subject in front of it.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What does Idris do at 6 o’clock? What does he do at 1 o’clock?
choices: At 6 o’clock he wakes up and gets ready. At 1 o’clock he eats lunch.
January is named first and December is named last.
They watched the sky, the moon and the trees.
rounds.answer: At 6 o’clock he wakes up and gets ready. At 1 o’clock he eats lunch.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: At 6 o’clock he wakes up and gets ready. At 1 o’clock he eats lunch.

rounds.prompt: The school year does not fill the whole calendar. Between which two months does it run?
choices: January is named first and December is named last.
She feels amazed, and the words that show it are: Amal’s eyes grew wide.
It runs from January to November.
rounds.answer: It runs from January to November.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: It runs from January to November.

rounds.prompt: Grandma Hana says people had no printed calendars a century ago. What did they watch instead of one?
choices: She means that long stretches of time pass more quickly than we expect.
They watched the sky, the moon and the trees.
It runs from January to November.
rounds.answer: They watched the sky, the moon and the trees.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: They watched the sky, the moon and the trees.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — My Daily Routine
Speaking 3 — When Is It?
Speaking 5 — Explain and Respond

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The school year ends in November.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: There are 24 hours in one day.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: In the future, I want to be a teacher.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'August' mean in this unit?
choices: The eighth month of the year, and a common time for family travel.
The first month of the year.
The second month of the year, and the shortest with twenty-eight days.
rounds.answer: The eighth month of the year, and a common time for family travel.
rounds.explanation: August: The eighth month of the year, and a common time for family travel.

rounds.prompt: What does 'century' mean in this unit?
choices: The first month of the year.
The second month of the year, and the shortest with twenty-eight days.
A period of one hundred years.
rounds.answer: A period of one hundred years.
rounds.explanation: century: A period of one hundred years.

rounds.prompt: What does 'February' mean in this unit?
choices: The fourth month of the year, when flowers bloom in many places.
The second month of the year, and the shortest with twenty-eight days.
The first month of the year.
rounds.answer: The second month of the year, and the shortest with twenty-eight days.
rounds.explanation: February: The second month of the year, and the shortest with twenty-eight days.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is '(December)'?
choices: Noun
Verb
Adjective
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: (December) is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'soon'?
choices: Noun
Adjective
Verb
rounds.answer: Verb
rounds.explanation: soon is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'May'?
choices: Adjective
Noun
Verb
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: May is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: The third month of the year, when heavy rains often arrive.
The fifth month of the year, which comes between April and June.
The seventh month of the year, often very warm.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: The ninth month of the year, when school often starts again.
The eleventh month of the year, after October and before December.
A paper or digital tool that shows the days, weeks and months of a year.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: A period of one hundred years.
The time that has not happened yet, coming after today.
Earlier than something else, coming before another time.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which month is sung straight after September in ‘The Months Song’?
choices: August.
October.
November.
December, the very last month of the year.
rounds.answer: October.
rounds.explanation: The song runs September, October, November, December, so October follows September.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing month from the story. “What month comes sooner — _____ or May?” asked Teacher Yasmin.
choices: April
August
October
November
rounds.answer: April
rounds.explanation: Teacher Yasmin compares the two months either side of each other, and Amal answers April.

rounds.prompt: What does the word century mean?
choices: A period of one hundred years.
A time when you stop school to rest.
The time that has not happened yet.
A chart on the wall that helps a family plan the whole year.
rounds.answer: A period of one hundred years.
rounds.explanation: Grandma Hana uses century to mean one hundred years. The others describe a vacation, the future and a calendar.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: What does the word calendar mean?
choices: A short rest from school or work.
A chart showing the days, weeks and months of one year.
A length of time that lasts sixty minutes.
A period of one hundred years that historians like to write about.
rounds.answer: A chart showing the days, weeks and months of one year.
rounds.explanation: A calendar is the chart itself. The other three describe a vacation, an hour and a century.

rounds.prompt: How many months does one full year have?
choices: Ten months.
Seven months.
Twelve months.
One hundred months altogether.
rounds.answer: Twelve months.
rounds.explanation: The reading The Twelve Months states it directly: one year has twelve months.

rounds.prompt: In ‘The Twelve Months’, when does the school year begin and end?
choices: It begins in January and ends in November.
It begins in March and ends in December.
It begins in June and ends in August.
It begins in September and finishes at the end of the following June.
rounds.answer: It begins in January and ends in November.
rounds.explanation: The text says the school year begins in January and ends in November, so December falls outside it.
